Workers read list elements, triggering Python reference counting writes on shared pages, which breaks fork COW. 9 python processes (parent + 8 workers) all reach ~599MB RSS each, exceeding 1GB cgroup limit

Call trace: filemap_fault -> __filemap_get_folio -> filemap_add_folio -> __filemap_add_folio -> __mem_cgroup_charge -> charge_memcg -> try_charge_memcg -> mem_cgroup_out_of_memory. OOM fires during page fault on mmap'd file page that was already evicted. pgscan_direct 412340 vs pgsteal_direct 205056 shows kernel scanned 412K pages but could only reclaim 205K (50% steal ratio). failcnt 212666 indicates 212K cgroup charge failures. file-rss:0kB in kill line despite 800MB mmap because all file-backed pages were reclaimed before OOM kill; anon-rss:519424kB (~507MB) = 97% of limit

Python fork bomb: parent process calls os.fork() in infinite loop, each child allocates 50MB via bytearray and touches all pages. In 1GB cgroup, ~20 children coexist before OOM. OOM kills one child, parent forks another, creating continuous kill-respawn cycle. failcnt 15191708 (15M failures) and 350 Killed processes in full dmesg confirm sustained thrashing. kernel 176783360 bytes (~168MB) of kernel overhead from process management (pagetables, kernel_stack, slab) is unusually high — slab_unreclaimable 111534304 (~106MB) from task_struct/mm_struct accumulation of rapid fork/kill cycles. Call trace: do_anonymous_page → __mem_cgroup_charge → try_charge_memcg → mem_cgroup_out_of_memory

log_env.reproduce_method: Based on LKML reports and Red Hat Bugzilla of mlx5_core order-3 allocation failures under memory fragmentation. mlx5 driver requires contiguous 32KB (order-3) pages for command buffers. Under heavy memory fragmentation (free pages exist but not contiguous), allocation fails with __GFP_NORETRY flag. User-space reproduction not feasible as this requires kernel buddy allocator fragmentation state.

log_env.vm_spec: NAS server, 64GB RAM, ZFS pool on 8x HDD
log_env.reproduce_method: Based on OpenZFS GitHub issues of ZFS ARC/L2ARC page allocation failures under fragmentation. ZFS ARC consumes memory via slab allocator (slab_reclaimable), and when ARC doesn't shrink fast enough under pressure, buddy allocator fragmentation causes order-2+ allocation failures for ZIO data buffers. User-space reproduction not feasible.

log_env.vm_spec: Container: NO memory limit. Host: ~7.7GB RAM, 2GB swap
log_env.reproduce_method: C++ std::vector<int> infinite push_back without reserve(). Vector capacity doubles on realloc (1→2→4→8GB), causing massive RSS growth until host memory exhausted.

log_env.reproduce_method: Elasticsearch fielddata cache OOM. Created index with fielddata:true on text field. Bulk inserted 100K documents with unique terms. Triggered terms aggregation with size=1000000 on text field, forcing fielddata loading of all unique terms into JVM heap. Fielddata cache unbounded (no indices.fielddata.cache.size set) combined with Xmx=600m in 1GB container causes OOM. Two java processes visible: ES main (pid 42648, ~950MB RSS) and ES controller (pid 42545, ~108MB RSS).

log_env.vm_spec: Container: NO memory limit. Host: ~7.7GB RAM, 2GB swap. V8: --max-old-space-size=16384
log_env.reproduce_method: Node.js V8 code cache exhaustion. Two-pronged memory growth: (1) new Function() called 5000 times per batch creates unique compiled code objects in V8 code cache (1.12M functions total), (2) Buffer.alloc(32MB) per batch creates external memory outside V8 heap (7GB+ external). --max-old-space-size=16384 prevents V8 internal OOM, forcing kernel global OOM. RSS reached 6.4GB with 7.1GB external memory at batch 224.
